SonicverseAudio operations platform
Product suite

Choose the workflow layer that fits your team right now.

Each Sonicverse product solves a clear operational job and can be adopted on its own or as part of a broader platform rollout.

Compare all productsScannable by use case, buyer, and operational outcome.
Streaming Infrastructure
AS
Audio Streaming StackView

A self-hostable delivery stack for broadcasters and audio products that need reliable ingest, failover, adaptive output, and operational visibility.

Get to a production-ready streaming baseline in days instead of months.
Developer API
MM
Media Metadata APIView

A unified API for reading, writing, and normalizing metadata across ingest, archive, and distribution workflows.

Stop maintaining one-off parsers and make metadata flows predictable.
Planning & Operations
BS
Broadcast SchedulerView

An open scheduling engine for live and automated radio playout that keeps planners, operators, and integrations aligned.

Replace brittle scheduling workflows with a system designed for modern broadcasting.

Developer API

Normalize metadata workflows before format drift slows every release.

Media Metadata API gives archive, platform, and publishing teams one programmable contract for reading, validating, and transforming media metadata.

Book a metadata walkthroughTalk to Sonicverse
Built for teams with real metadata sprawl

Replace one-off parsers, reduce field mismatches, and make publishing rules easier to maintain across systems.

Unified schema layerMap multiple metadata formats into one stable operational contract.

The hero visual leans technical on purpose: this product wins when teams immediately see abstraction, validation, and consistency.

InputID3 / Vorbis / MP4 / RSS

Read and write multiple format families behind one shared API.

Format abstraction
ValidationNormalize fields before they fork across systems

Centralize transformation and validation rules instead of duplicating them in each tool.

Batch-safe automation
OutputReturn release-ready metadata payloads

Feed archives, players, apps, and syndication systems with cleaner contracts.

Publishing consistency

Built for pipeline teams

A single interface for metadata that usually lives in four different systems.

When metadata changes shape at every step of the workflow, releases slow down and quality issues multiply. This API centralizes the hard parts.

01

Format abstraction

Read and write multiple audio metadata formats through one normalized contract.

02

Batch-safe automation

Process large media libraries with predictable validation and transformation rules.

03

Podcast-aware fields

Handle episode type, season data, chapters, and other distribution-specific fields cleanly.

04

Cleaner integrations

Reduce edge-case logic in publishing systems, archives, and third-party platform adapters.

Where it fits

Use it as a shared metadata layer across ingest, curation, and distribution.

Instead of every product team maintaining its own metadata rules, you can expose one stable service and evolve it centrally.

Ideal teams

Best suited to engineering teams with multiple metadata touchpoints or legacy format fragmentation.

Ingest

Normalize incoming files before they touch storage or editorial tooling.

Archive

Keep long-term media libraries searchable and structurally consistent.

Publishing

Push release-ready payloads into feeds, players, apps, and syndication systems.

Want to remove metadata debt?

Let’s map your current pipeline and show where a shared API helps most.

We’ll identify the biggest duplication points and outline a staged rollout that does not interrupt publishing.

Book a metadata walkthroughTalk to Sonicverse
SonicverseAudio operations platform

Sonicverse is the modular audio operations platform for teams that want enterprise-grade workflows without proprietary lock-in.
